title: Why Communication Matters
body:
Communication matters because misunderstanding is costly. It creates mistakes, frustration, conflict, and wasted time across work, family, and public life.

It also matters because clear communication creates leverage. A person who can explain, persuade, and listen well can often outperform someone with more raw knowledge.

In public life, communication is how institutions build trust. In private life, it is how people stay connected without constantly guessing what the other person meant.

Almost every serious field depends on this topic in practice.
keyPoints: Listening is as important as speaking
furtherReading: Made to Stick by Chip and Dan Heath
On Writing Well by William Zinsser
The Pyramid Principle by Barbara Minto
